---
title: "BAA1104: E-Portfolio and Business Analytics Challenges - Reflection"
---

## Looking Back

Prior to this module, I had never heard of Quarto and had no idea what Git or GitHub was. To be completely honest - the first couple of 
weeks were tough. I found myself constantly breaking things, discovering the error and then breaking again. This process was frustrating and unrewarding
at times however, I believe this is how it all clicked for me.

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
My best learning in this module occured when things went wrong, not when they worked first time. This trial and error approach allowed me to find the reasons *why* errors happen
and how I could apply this going forward.
:::


---

## My Biggest Surprise

Realising that I could build my own personal portfolio and one that I am genuinely proud of was somewhat unexpected. 
Having the ability to own, control and keep building on websites was something I thought only strong developers had.
Despite only beginning my path in this journey, I now feel I have something valuable that represents me as an overall individual -
something regular college assignments struggle to provide.


---

## The Classes

The interactive and engaging manner of classes really suited me as an analytics beginner.
Trying things, making mistakes and figuring out solutions was the overall process throughout this project and 
I believe this was rooted in the dynamic lecture style promoting encouragement and acceptance of first-time failure.
Being surrounded by students of a similar level enabled teamwork and collaborative gain within the classroom setting and 
made technical challenges feel less intimidating than facing them alone.

---

## 🔄 What I Would Do Differently


| Area | What I'd Do Differently |
|---|---|
| Git | Start using it properly from day one, completing a beginner course before week one |
| Python Setup | Set up Jupyter and environments before the semester and not during classes |
| Project Structure | Plan the folder layout upfront -reorganising later leads to lost time and confusion|
| Commits | Make them smaller and more regularly rather than large pushes, allows visible website development |

::: {.callout-tip}
💡 **For anyone starting out** — this video was a great starting point for understanding Git:

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGOj5yH7evk >}}
:::

---

## 📊 Skills Developed in This Module

| Skill | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Quarto | Never heard of it | Can build and deploy a full site |
| Git & GitHub | Unused | Regular commits and GitHub Pages |
| Python in documents | Coding only in notebooks | Executable chunks on a published website |
| Web publishing | No experience | Live site on GitHub Pages |
| Professional portfolio | CV only | Multi-page portfolio with live projects and personal aspect |

---

## Where This Leaves Me

I'm a trainee accountant who can now build and deploy live data portfolios. This combination
of financial knowledge and technical capabilities is exactly where I want to be heading. This module has pushed 
me further along this desired path. Continuing into the future, my portfolio presents clearly who I am, what I know and
how I contribute value to others.

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
Going forward, I intend to keep this portfolio updated as I complete my ACCA 
qualification, develop new projects, and grow in my role at the Mercantile Group. Along with my [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ruairi-c-02b268130/){target="_blank"}, this will
further increase my digital presence.
:::

---

::: {.callout-note}
Thank you for reading. You can explore my projects on the [Projects page](projects/index.qmd) 
or [get in touch](contact.qmd) if you'd like to connect.
:::
